description: Evaluation test set for comparing 3 agentic search approaches on DeepAgents documentation

test_cases.question: What is the purpose of subagents in DeepAgents and when should you NOT use them?

key_points: Subagents solve context bloat problem by isolating detailed work
Main agent receives only final result, not intermediate tool calls
Context quarantine - keeps main agent context clean
Should NOT use for simple single-step tasks
Should NOT use when you need to maintain intermediate context
Should NOT use when overhead outweighs benefits

reference_chunks.content_preview: Subagents solve the context bloat problem... When NOT to use subagents: Simple single-step tasks, When you need to maintain intermediate context...

test_cases.question: How do you configure long-term memory that persists across conversation threads in DeepAgents?

key_points: Use CompositeBackend with path routing
Route /memories/ path to StoreBackend
Default paths go to StateBackend (ephemeral)
Requires a LangGraph Store (InMemoryStore for dev, PostgresStore for production)
Files with /memories/ prefix persist across threads

reference_chunks.content_preview: Configure long-term memory by using a CompositeBackend that routes the /memories/ path to a StoreBackend...

test_cases.question: What are the three types of decisions a human can make when reviewing a tool call in human-in-the-loop mode?

key_points: approve - execute tool with original arguments
edit - modify tool arguments before execution
reject - skip executing the tool call entirely

reference_chunks.content_preview: approve: Execute the tool with the original arguments... edit: Modify the tool arguments... reject: Skip executing this tool call entirely

test_cases.question: What is the difference between skills and tools in DeepAgents, and when should you use each?

key_points: Skills provide specialized workflows and domain knowledge via SKILL.md files
Skills use progressive disclosure - only loaded when needed
Use skills when there is a lot of context to reduce tokens in system prompt
Use skills to bundle capabilities together into larger actions
Use tools if agent does not have access to file system

reference_chunks.content_preview: Use skills when there is a lot of context... Use skills to bundle capabilities together... Use tools if the agent does not have access to the file system

test_cases.question: If I want to create a research agent that delegates web searches to a subagent AND saves research findings to persistent memory across sessions, what specific DeepAgents components would I need to configure and how do they work together?
reasoning_chain: Step 1: Identify subagent configuration for web search delegation
Step 2: Identify long-term memory configuration for persistence
Step 3: Combine both in a single agent setup
Step 4: Explain interaction between subagent results and memory storage

key_points: Create a research subagent with name, description, system_prompt, and tools (web search)
Pass subagents list to create_deep_agent
Configure CompositeBackend with StateBackend as default
Route /memories/ to StoreBackend for persistence
Provide a LangGraph Store (PostgresStore for production)
Instruct subagent to return concise summaries (not raw search results)
Main agent can save subagent results to /memories/research/ for persistence
Use checkpointer for session management

reference_chunks.content_preview: Return concise results... Do NOT include raw data, intermediate calculations...

chunk_hit_rate.description: Percentage of reference chunks that were retrieved/consulted

response_score.0: Poor - Missing most key points, incorrect information, or irrelevant
response_score.1: Adequate - Covers some key points but incomplete or partially incorrect
response_score.2: Excellent - Covers all/most key points accurately with good completeness

time_to_complete.description: Wall clock time from query submission to final response
